    feat(server): operatorPromptSubmit GraphQL mutation with CSRF + idempotency + identity audit [ddx-f171a715]
    
    - New mutation operatorPromptSubmit(OperatorPromptSubmitInput!) → OperatorPromptSubmitResult
      in cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql
    - Resolver in resolver_operator_prompt.go validates X-CSRF-Token header against
      a per-process StaticCSRFTokenStore (rejects with a 403-tagged error otherwise)
    - Process-local MemoryIdempotencyCache deduplicates submissions within a 24h
      TTL window; duplicate keys return the original bead with deduplicated=true
    - Audit captured by appending a BeadEvent of kind operator_prompt_submit on
      the proposed bead, recording originating identity (X-Tailscale-User for
      ts-net peers, RemoteAddr for localhost) plus the idempotency key
    - Created beads default to status=proposed via bead.NewOperatorPromptBead
    - Server wires the CSRF store and idempotency cache once at New() and plumbs
      the originating *http.Request into resolver context via WithHTTPRequest
    - Tests in resolver_operator_prompt_test.go cover all five AC items: schema
      presence, CSRF reject/accept, 24h dedup window, audit-event identity, and
      the proposed default status

121		}

153	// NewOperatorPromptBead constructs a fresh operator-prompt bead from a raw
154	// prompt string, applying the Story 15 template defaults: title is the first
155	// non-empty line of the prompt, full prompt body is preserved verbatim in the
156	// description, default labels are kind:operator-prompt + source:web-ui, the
157	// status starts in `proposed` (approval flow), priority defaults to the
158	// caller-supplied tier (clamped to MinPriority..MaxPriority), and the
159	// acceptance field carries the auto-AC stub.

162	// will assign the ID and CreatedAt/UpdatedAt timestamps.
163	func NewOperatorPromptBead(prompt string, defaultTier int) *Bead {
164		body := strings.TrimSpace(prompt)

167			title = strings.TrimSpace(body[:i])
168		}
169		if title == "" {

171		}
172		tier := defaultTier

175		}
176		if tier > MaxPriority {

178		}

"""Submit an operator prompt as a proposed bead. Requires a valid CSRF token via the X-CSRF-Token request header (HTTP transport), and an idempotency key that dedupes repeated submissions within a 24-hour window. The resulting bead is in the proposed status (not auto-queued for execution); audit captures the originating identity (localhost user OR ts-net WhoIs)."""
  operatorPromptSubmit(input: OperatorPromptSubmitInput!): OperatorPromptSubmitResult!

  """Approve a proposed operator-prompt bead, transitioning it from proposed → open so the execute-loop can claim it. Requires a valid CSRF token. The originating identity must be on the per-project auto-approve allowlist; ts-net identities are never permitted (locked decision). An audit event of kind operator_prompt_approve records the approver. Errors when the bead is missing, is not an operator-prompt, is not in the proposed status, or the caller is not on the allowlist."""
  operatorPromptApprove(id: ID!): OperatorPromptApproveResult!

  """Cancel a proposed operator-prompt bead, transitioning it from proposed → cancelled so it never enters the execute-loop. Requires a valid CSRF token. Any trusted identity (localhost OR ts-net) may cancel a proposal — the allowlist gates auto-approval, not rejection. An audit event of kind operator_prompt_cancel records the canceller. Errors when the bead is missing, is not an operator-prompt, or is not in the proposed status."""
  operatorPromptCancel(id: ID!): OperatorPromptCancelResult!
}

input OperatorPromptSubmitInput {

  tier: Int

  idempotencyKey: String!
}

type OperatorPromptSubmitResult {

  bead: Bead!
  """True if the request matched a prior idempotency-key submission within 24h."""
  deduplicated: Boolean!
}

"""Input for operatorPromptSubmit. The prompt body is preserved verbatim in the bead description; tier is clamped to the bead priority range; idempotencyKey dedupes repeat submissions within a 24-hour window."""
input OperatorPromptSubmitInput {
  """The full operator prompt body. The first non-empty line becomes the bead title; the entire body is stored as the bead description."""
  prompt: String!
  """Optional priority tier for the resulting bead (clamped to the valid bead priority range). Defaults to 2 when omitted."""
  tier: Int
  """Idempotency key. Repeat submissions with the same key within a 24-hour window return the original bead unchanged (deduplicated=true)."""
  idempotencyKey: String!
  """When true, ask the server to immediately approve (proposed → open) the resulting bead so it enters the execute-loop without a separate operatorPromptApprove call. The server enforces the per-project auto-approve allowlist: only configured-localhost identities are eligible — ts-net identities are NEVER auto-approved (locked decision). When the caller is not eligible the bead is still created in the proposed status and autoApproved is false."""
  autoApprove: Boolean
}

"""Result of operatorPromptSubmit. When deduplicated=true, the request matched an idempotency key submitted within the last 24 hours and the original bead is returned unchanged."""
type OperatorPromptSubmitResult {
  """The proposed operator-prompt bead."""
  bead: Bead!
  """True if the request matched a prior idempotency-key submission within 24h."""
  deduplicated: Boolean!
  """True when the bead was auto-approved (status=open) rather than left in the proposed status. Only set on first submission for callers on the per-project auto-approve allowlist."""
  autoApproved: Boolean!
}

"""Result of operatorPromptApprove. The bead has transitioned from proposed → open and an audit event has been appended."""
type OperatorPromptApproveResult {
  """The bead after approval (status=open)."""
  bead: Bead!
}

"""Result of operatorPromptCancel. The bead has transitioned from proposed → cancelled and an audit event has been appended."""
type OperatorPromptCancelResult {
  """The bead after cancellation (status=cancelled)."""
  bead: Bead!
}

command_groups:
  go_checks: &go_checks
    root: "cli/"  # Go module root
    tags: [go, backend]

  security_checks: &security_checks
    tags: [security]

  formatting_checks: &formatting_checks
    tags: [format, style]

# ====================
# Pre-commit hooks - Fast checks on staged files
# ====================
pre-commit:
  parallel: true

  commands:
    # Conflict detection using grep on staged files only.
    # Uses git diff --cached directly to avoid {staged_files} tty/expansion issues
    # when running detached (e.g. ddx work --local, no controlling tty).
    conflicts:

name: "Check merge conflicts"
      run: |
        files=$(git diff --cached --name-only --diff-filter=ACMR 2>/dev/null)
        [ -z "$files" ] && exit 0
        conflict_file=$(printf '%s\n' "$files" | while IFS= read -r f; do
          [ -f "$f" ] || continue
          grep -lE "^<<<<<<<[ 	]|^=======$|^>>>>>>>[ 	]|^\|{7}[ 	]" "$f" 2>/dev/null && break
        done | head -1)
        if [ -n "$conflict_file" ]; then
          echo "Conflict markers found in: $conflict_file"
--
    #   exclude: "*_test.go"
    #   run: |
    #     if command -v rg >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    #       rg -q "(fmt\.Print|println|spew\.Dump|pprof\.|debug\.Print)" {staged_files} && exit 1 || exit 0
    #     else
    #       grep -E "(fmt\.Print|println|spew\.Dump|pprof\.|debug\.Print)" {staged_files} 2>/dev/null && exit 1 || exit 0
    #     fi
    #   fail_text: "Debug statements found in Go files"

    debug-python:
--
      glob: "*.py"
      run: |
        if command -v rg >/dev/null 2>&1; then
          rg -q "(print\(|pdb\.set_trace|breakpoint\(\)|import pdb)" {staged_files} && exit 1 || exit 0
        else
          grep -E "(print\(|pdb\.set_trace|breakpoint\(\)|import pdb)" {staged_files} 2>/dev/null && exit 1 || exit 0
        fi
      fail_text: "Debug statements found in Python files"

    # Go formatting - native gofmt
--
      glob: "cli/**/*.go"
      run: |
        unformatted=$(gofmt -l {staged_files})
        if [ -n "$unformatted" ]; then
          echo "Files need formatting: $unformatted"
          echo "Run: gofmt -w <files>"
          exit 1
        fi
      skip:
        - merge
--
      glob: "cli/**/*.go"
      run: |
        if ! command -v golangci-lint >/dev/null 2>&1; then
          echo "❌ golangci-lint is required but not installed"
          echo "Run: scripts/dev-setup.sh"
          exit 1
        fi
        # Run on packages containing staged files, not individual files.
        # Skip Go testdata directories — these contain analyzer fixtures
        # that intentionally reference stub packages and only typecheck
--
      glob: "cli/**/*.go"
      run: 'go build -v ./...'
      skip:
        - merge
        - rebase

    # FEAT-022 evidence-lint: structural sink coverage for the
    # no-unbounded-prompts invariant. Scoped to packages containing
    # staged files, with diagnostics filtered to only those staged
    # files so existing violations in untouched packages don't block
--
      glob: "cli/**/*.go"
      run: |
        staged=$(git diff --cached --name-only --diff-filter=ACMR | grep -E '^cli/.+\.go$' || true)
        [ -z "$staged" ] && exit 0
        pkgs=$(printf '%s\n' "$staged" | xargs -n1 dirname | sort -u | sed 's|^cli/|./|' | grep -v '^\./tools/lint/evidencelint' || true)
        [ -z "$pkgs" ] && exit 0
        out=$(go run ./tools/lint/evidencelint/cmd/evidencelint $pkgs 2>&1) || true
        violations=""
        for f in $staged; do
          bare="${f#cli/}"
--
      glob: "cli/**/*.go"
      run: |
        staged=$(git diff --cached --name-only --diff-filter=ACMR | grep -E '^cli/.+\.go$' || true)

go-test:
      <<: *go_checks
      name: "Go Tests"
      glob: "cli/**/*.go"
      run: |
        packages=$(echo {staged_files} | xargs -n1 dirname | sort -u | sed 's|^cli/||')
        for pkg in $packages; do
          if ls "$pkg"/*_test.go >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            go test -short -race "./$pkg"
          fi
        done
      skip:
        - merge
